first_paragraph: **Larissa Hope Wilson**[[1]](#cite_note-1) (born 5 May 1989) is an English actress and director, best known for her role as [Jal Fazer](/wiki/Jal_Fazer "Jal Fazer") in the first two series of the UK television series *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* (2007-2008). Other credits include *[Holby City](/wiki/Holby_City "Holby City")* (2008), *[Kingdom](/wiki/Kingdom_(2007_TV_series) "Kingdom (2007 TV series)")* (2009), *[Tormented](/wiki/Tormented_(2009_British_film) "Tormented (2009 British film)")* (2009). *[Sparticle Mystery](/wiki/Sparticle_Mystery "Sparticle Mystery")* (2011), *[The Town](/wiki/The_Town_(2012_TV_series) "The Town (2012 TV series)")* (2012), *[Trollied](/wiki/Trollied "Trollied")* (2012), and *[Suspects](/wiki/Suspects_(TV_series) "Suspects (TV series)")* (2014).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English actress

| Larissa Wilson | |
| --- | --- |
| Wilson in 2007 | |
| Born | Larissa Hope Wilson   (1989-05-05) 5 May 1989 (age 36)  [Kingswood](/wiki/Kingswood,_South_Gloucestershire "Kingswood, South Gloucestershire"), [South Gloucestershire](/wiki/South_Gloucestershire "South Gloucestershire"), England |
| Occupation | Actress |
| Years active | 2007–2015, 2020–present |
| Children | 1 |

**Larissa Hope Wilson**[[1]](#cite_note-1) (born 5 May 1989) is an English actress and director, best known for her role as [Jal Fazer](/wiki/Jal_Fazer "Jal Fazer") in the first two series of the UK television series *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* (2007-2008). Other credits include *[Holby City](/wiki/Holby_City "Holby City")* (2008), *[Kingdom](/wiki/Kingdom_(2007_TV_series) "Kingdom (2007 TV series)")* (2009), *[Tormented](/wiki/Tormented_(2009_British_film) "Tormented (2009 British film)")* (2009). *[Sparticle Mystery](/wiki/Sparticle_Mystery "Sparticle Mystery")* (2011), *[The Town](/wiki/The_Town_(2012_TV_series) "The Town (2012 TV series)")* (2012), *[Trollied](/wiki/Trollied "Trollied")* (2012), and *[Suspects](/wiki/Suspects_(TV_series) "Suspects (TV series)")* (2014).

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Larissa_Wilson&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Wilson was working at [New Look](/wiki/New_Look_(company) "New Look (company)"), and auditioned for *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* during her lunch break at [Oceana (nightclub)](/wiki/Oceana_(nightclub) "Oceana (nightclub)") in [Bristol](/wiki/Bristol "Bristol").[[2]](#cite_note-water-2) Wilson graduated from university with a First Class Degree in Creative Writing and Psychology.[[2]](#cite_note-water-2)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Larissa_Wilson&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Wilson made her acting debut in 2007 at age 17 when she starred as [Jal Fazer](/wiki/Jal_Fazer "Jal Fazer") in the British teen drama *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")*, alongside [Nicholas Hoult](/wiki/Nicholas_Hoult "Nicholas Hoult"), [Hannah Murray](/wiki/Hannah_Murray "Hannah Murray"), and [Dev Patel](/wiki/Dev_Patel "Dev Patel").[[3]](#cite_note-skins-3)

She appeared in *[Holby City](/wiki/Holby_City "Holby City")* (2008),[[4]](#cite_note-tv-4) *[Kingdom](/wiki/Kingdom_(2007_TV_series) "Kingdom (2007 TV series)")* (2009),[[4]](#cite_note-tv-4) and was reunited with *Skins* co-star [April Pearson](/wiki/April_Pearson "April Pearson") in the British film *[Tormented](/wiki/Tormented_(2009_British_film) "Tormented (2009 British film)")* (2009).[[4]](#cite_note-tv-4)

She has appeared in *[Sparticle Mystery](/wiki/Sparticle_Mystery "Sparticle Mystery")* (2011),[[4]](#cite_note-tv-4) *[The Town](/wiki/The_Town_(2012_TV_series) "The Town (2012 TV series)")* (2012),[[4]](#cite_note-tv-4) *[Trollied](/wiki/Trollied "Trollied")* (2012),[[5]](#cite_note-trolley-5) and *[Suspects](/wiki/Suspects_(TV_series) "Suspects (TV series)")* (2014).[[4]](#cite_note-tv-4)

In 2020 she starred in the [BBC Sounds](/wiki/BBC_Sounds "BBC Sounds") podcast series *Murmurs*.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

She made her directorial debut in 2020 with the short film *SHIFT*.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
